text:
You are working on beads issue str-ol3: "Stage 1 (Observe): Execute and trace collection"

## Issue Details
Run function with many inputs (random, boundary, user-provided), record execution traces, no Z3 involvement. Independent module with clear input/output interface (inputs → execution traces). This stage alone provides value for users who want to understand execution coverage without constraint solving.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="str-ol3"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Instructions
1. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
2. Read existing explorer.rs and orchestrator.rs to understand how execution and trace collection currently works
3. Create a new observe module (or extend existing) with a clear input/output interface
4. Input: function info, list of inputs (random, boundary, user-provided)
5. Output: execution traces with branch coverage data
6. No Z3 dependency — pure execution and observation
7. Add unit tests and proptest coverage
8. Run quality gates: `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` in shatter-core
9. Run `/pre-completion` before reporting done — include its output in your completion message
10. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`

When done, mark task #5 as completed via TaskUpdate and send a message to the team lead.

Run /pre-completion before reporting done.","assignedBy":"str-ol3","timestamp":"2026-03-07T23:33:44.183Z"}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772926475125-epftwml","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"REPO_ROOT=\"$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)\" && BRANCH_NAME=\"str-ol3\" && WORKTREE_DIR=\"$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME\" && if [ -d \"$WORKTREE_DIR\" ]; then echo \"Worktree already exists, reusing\"; else git worktree add \"$WORKTREE_DIR\" -b \"$BRANCH_NAME\"; fi && cd \"$WORKTREE_DIR\" && echo \"CWD: $(pwd)\" && echo \"Branch: $(git branch --show-current)\" && echo \"$(pwd)\" | grep -q '\\.claude/worktrees/' && echo \"WORKTREE VERIFIED\" || echo \"FATAL: NOT in a worktree\"","description":"Set up git worktree for str-ol3"},"permission_updates":[]}}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772927358283-m8j5l6b","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add observe module — Stage 1 execute and trace collection

New `observe.rs` module provides a composable building block for executing
a function with pre-generated inputs and collecting execution traces without
Z3 or constraint solving. Clear input/output interface: inputs in, traces out.

Key additions:
- `observe_function()`: full lifecycle (instrument → setup → execute → teardown)
- `observe_batch()`: low-level batch execution without lifecycle management
- `ObserveConfig`: focused config excluding input generation concerns
- `BatchObservation`: lightweight trace collection result
- `From<&ExploreConfig>` for easy integration with existing code
- Unit tests and proptest coverage for core invariants

Also makes `frontend_supports`, `send_setup`, `send_teardown` pub(crate) in
explorer.rs for cross-module reuse.

Closes: str-ol3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"","description":"Commit observe module"},"permission_updates":[]}}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772935669848-wazszj2","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"GIT_SSH_COMMAND=\"ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no\" git push -u origin HEAD 2>&1 || git push -u origin HEAD --force-with-lease 2>&1 || echo \"Push failed - SSH key issue\"","description":"Try push with explicit SSH key"},"permission_updates":[]}}
